operations.name: Endpoint triage — pick which to surface
operations.trigger: Wrapping a new API as agent tools; API has >3 endpoints
operations.action: List every endpoint × CRUD verb. For each: ask 'what *user intent* would the agent be solving?' Drop endpoints that have no agent-facing intent (admin, batch jobs, webhooks-out). Output: ≤10 candidates worth surfacing.
operations.output: Triaged endpoint list with intent labels

operations.name: Name tools by intent, not by REST verb
operations.action: Tool name = verb_object describing the user/agent intent: `search_orders`, `cancel_order`, `get_order_status` — NOT `post_orders_v2`, `delete_orders_id`, `get_orders_id`. Test: a non-API-savvy LM reading only the name should guess when to call it.

operations.name: Define typed input schema (Pydantic / JSON Schema)
operations.action: Pydantic model with type-annotated fields. Every field gets a `description=` aimed at the LM (units, format, enum values, examples). Required vs Optional explicit. Flatten nested API params (e.g. `filter[status]=open` → `status: Literal['open','closed']`).

operations.name: Inject auth at wrapper boundary, never in args
operations.trigger: Tool needs API key / OAuth token
operations.action: Read secret from env var or secret store **inside** the wrapper. Never expose `api_key` as a tool parameter — the LM doesn't need to see it and may echo it in traces. Per-tenant tokens: pass via closure or context object, not via tool args.
operations.output: Tool that authenticates without secret in schema

operations.name: Wrap with timeout + retry + jittered backoff
operations.trigger: Any outbound HTTP call from a tool
operations.action: httpx/requests with explicit `timeout=` (per-attempt). Retry only on 429/5xx/network errors, max 3-5 attempts, exponential backoff with jitter. Read `Retry-After` header when present. Never retry 4xx other than 408/429.

operations.name: Handle pagination — cursor by default
operations.action: Default: tool fetches one page, returns items + `next_cursor` in response. Let the agent decide to call again. Auto-loop only if max items is small (≤200) and bounded. Never auto-loop unbounded.
operations.output: Paginated tool with explicit cursor surface

operations.name: Shape response — trim to LM-relevant fields
operations.trigger: API returns large JSON (>2 KB or nested deep)
operations.action: Define an output Pydantic model with only fields the agent needs to reason or act on. Drop audit timestamps, internal IDs, deprecated mirrors. Return string summaries for blobs. Keep <2 KB per call when possible.

operations.name: Translate errors into actionable LM feedback
operations.trigger: API returns non-2xx, or local exception
operations.action: Catch HTTPStatusError / ValidationError. Return a structured error: `{error: 'rate_limited' | 'not_found' | 'invalid_input' | 'auth_failed' | 'server_error', message: '...', retryable: bool, hint: '...'}`. Never raise raw stack traces back to the LM.

operations.name: Mark mutations with idempotency keys
operations.action: Generate idempotency key (UUID v4 derived from logical operation) per call. Pass via `Idempotency-Key` header if API supports it. Tag the tool in metadata: `mutating=True, idempotent=True/False`. Cross-link to T4 (HITL/approval) for non-idempotent mutations.

operations.name: Wire into framework: @tool / Tool class / MCP
operations.trigger: Tool implementation done; deploying into agent
operations.action: LangChain/LangGraph: `@tool` decorator with Pydantic args_schema. CrewAI: subclass `BaseTool` with `_run`. OpenAI: `tools=[{type:'function', function:{...}}]`. Anthropic: `tools=[{name,description,input_schema}]`. MCP server: `@mcp.tool()`. Same Pydantic schema → all targets via `model_json_schema()`.

anti_patterns_summary: AP-1 1:1 endpoint→tool mapping (50 tiny tools, LM gets lost)
AP-2 Raw JSON blob returned to LM (context bloat, hallucinated field names)
AP-3 Secrets/tokens exposed in tool description or args (leak in traces)
AP-4 No rate-limit / retry handling (single 429 kills the run)
AP-5 Unbounded pagination auto-loop (token blowout, OOM)
AP-6 Procedural tool description ('First call X then Y') (treat tool as function, not a script)
dilemma_cases_summary: DC-1 Wide API (50+ endpoints): one mega-tool with `operation` param vs many small tools
DC-2 API returns 10 MB JSON: how much to expose to LM
DC-3 Async/long-running API (job → poll): single tool with internal wait vs two tools (submit + poll)

key_quotes.text: A mega-tool with a single instructions string invites hallucinations.
Return machine-readable rate limit headers that agents can use to self-throttle.
Cursor-based pagination is more reliable than offset/limit for agentic scrolling.
Tool descriptions are often more important than code comments because the LLM directly uses them for reasoning.
